mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate (below 5%) reflects a tight labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings and equity prices higher over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year rank IC of UNRATE (inverted) with 20-day forward returns is below 0.05, or a simple linear regression slope is not significantly negative at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0% during a tightening cycle, it increases the discount rate for equities and reduces liquidity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: In triggered instances (FEDFUNDS>2.0%), the mean 20-day forward return is not significantly negative at 5% level (one-sided t-test), or fewer than 55% of triggered instances are followed by negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate broad demand expansion, which directly boosts corporate revenues and equity valuations; the synergistic effect is stronger than either series alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term (product of normalized HOUST and RSAFS) has a rank IC below 0.03 with 60-day returns over any 2-year rolling window, or the interaction term adds no significant predictive power beyond individual series in a nested model test (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions (more than 6 years into recovery), a two-month consecutive decline in industrial production signals approaching recession, which depresses forward equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for two consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion (7+ years from trough as of 2018)
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, fewer than 60% of subsequent 20-day SPX returns are negative, or the average return is not significantly below zero (one-sided t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly acceleration in consumer price inflation (greater than 0.3% month-over-month) raises expectations of tighter monetary policy, which pressures equity valuations in the short term over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered events, the 5-day forward return is not significantly negative (p>0.05) or the proportion of negative returns is less than 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in both industrial production and nonfarm payrolls indicates a healthy economy with robust output and employment, predicting positive equity returns over a quarter horizon; the combined signal is more reliable than either individually.
failure_condition: The interaction term's rank IC with 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the composite signal fails to outperform a simple average of the individual series' signals.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales reflect strong consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The rank IC of RSAFS month-over-month growth with 20-day forward returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year window, or the average return in top-quintile RSAFS growth months is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts (greater than 10%) signals a sudden drop in residential investment and consumer sentiment, leading to negative equity returns in the following week.
failure_condition: In triggered events, fewer than 55% of 5-day forward returns are negative, or the mean return is not significantly below zero (p>0.05).
